message:
Add hooks config loader and Manager lifecycle tests (44.6%→67.1%)

Tests for LoadCommandHooksFromMainConfig, LoadHooksFromDirectory,
LoadCommandHooks (read error, parse error, validation error paths),
and full Manager lifecycle using temp git repos: NewManager, Install,
Uninstall, Status, ListAll, RepoRoot, HooksDir, findGitRoot,
generateHookScript.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -211,6 +211,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1qct","title":"Feature: Progress summary in status/dashboard/robot","description":"## Overview
Surface a **progress summary** (beads completed vs assigned) in existing outputs rather than adding a new standalone progress command.

## Scope
- Add progress bar/ratio to `ntm status` and dashboard
- Add fields to `--robot-status` / `--robot-dashboard`
- Compute using assignment tracking + BV closed/ready counts

## Tests
- Unit: progress calculation logic
- E2E: assign beads → close beads → verify progress updates in status + robot output

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-PROGRESS] assigned=%d completed=%d ratio=%.2f", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Progress visible without new CLI entry
- Robot output includes progress fields
- Deterministic outputs for same state","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:31.268447007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:35:43.870120308Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qct","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qfho","title":"Task: Implement ensemble dashboard keymap + shortcuts","description":"Implement keyboard shortcuts for ensemble dashboard navigation and synthesis control.

## Keyboard Shortcut Map

### Global Shortcuts
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| q | Quit dashboard |
| ? | Show help overlay |
| r | Refresh status |
| Esc | Close overlay/unfocus |

### Mode Navigation
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| j/↓ | Move to next mode |
| k/↑ | Move to previous mode |
| Enter | Zoom to selected window |
| Tab | Cycle focus between panels |

### Synthesis Control
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| s | Start synthesis (when ready) |
| S | Force synthesis (even if incomplete) |
| e | Export synthesis result |
| c | Copy synthesis to clipboard |

### View Controls
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| 1-9 | Jump to window N |
| [ | Decrease panel width |
| ] | Increase panel width |
| f | Toggle fullscreen mode panel |
| v | Toggle verbose mode info |

## Implementation
```go
// internal/tui/ensemble_keys.go
type EnsembleKeyMap struct {
    Quit           key.Binding
    Help           key.Binding
    Refresh        key.Binding
    NextMode       key.Binding
    PrevMode       key.Binding
    ZoomWindow     key.Binding
    CycleFocus     key.Binding
    StartSynthesis key.Binding
    ForceSynthesis key.Binding
    Export         key.Binding
    Copy           key.Binding
    JumpWindow     []key.Binding
}

func DefaultEnsembleKeyMap() EnsembleKeyMap
func (k EnsembleKeyMap) ShortHelp() []key.Binding
func (k EnsembleKeyMap) FullHelp() [][]key.Binding

// Key handler in main model
func (m EnsembleDashboard) handleKeyPress(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd)
```

### Help Overlay
Show formatted help when ? is pressed.

## File Location
internal/tui/ensemble_keys.go

## Dependencies
- Ensemble panel (bd-121pa)

## Logging Requirements
- Log key actions in debug mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T10:30:21.446232608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:12.614732938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:12.614641227Z","close_reason":"EnsembleKeyMap struct, DefaultEnsembleKeyMap(), ShortHelp(), FullHelp(), JumpWindow bindings all implemented in ensemble_keys.go. Comprehensive tests in ensemble_keys_test.go.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qfho","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qfho","depends_on_id":"bd-rylze","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qls","title":"Add agent naming - give agents memorable names instead of pane numbers (e.g., claude-alpha, codex-bravo)","description":"Add agent naming - give agents memorable names instead of pane numbers.

## Problem Being Solved

Referring to agents by pane number (cc:2, cc:3) is confusing. Names like 'claude-alpha', 'codex-bravo' are more memorable.

## Naming Scheme

### Default Names
- CC agents: claude-alpha, claude-bravo, claude-charlie, ...
- Codex agents: codex-alpha, codex-bravo, ...
- Gemini agents: gemini-alpha, gemini-bravo, ...

### Custom Names
```bash
ntm spawn my_session --cc=2 --names "reviewer,implementer"
```

## Display Integration

### Session List
```
my_session (3 agents)
  claude-alpha (pane 2) - active
  claude-bravo (pane 3) - active  
  codex-charlie (pane 4) - idle
```

### Logs/Watch
```
[claude-alpha] Starting implementation...
[codex-charlie] Reviewing changes...
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-agent-names=SESSION

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "agents": [
    {"name": "claude-alpha", "pane": 2, "type": "cc"},
    {"name": "claude-bravo", "pane": 3, "type": "cc"}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Agents get default names based on type (claude-alpha, etc.)
2. --names flag allows custom agent names
3. Names displayed in session list
4. Names used in logs output
5. Robot mode returns name-to-pane mapping
6. Names persist across session lifetime

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/naming/naming_test.go
- Test default name generation
- Test custom name assignment

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-AGENT-NAMES] Agent Naming Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-AGENT-NAMES]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing agent naming"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Agents get memorable names instead of pane numbers
2. Names like claude-alpha, codex-bravo generated
3. Names unique within session
4. Names preserved across restarts
5. Robot mode outputs include agent names
6. Unit tests for name generation
7. E2E test verifying names persist","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1qsp","title":"Task: Create CostTracker module","description":"## Purpose
Track estimated API costs across all agents in a session.

## CostTracker Struct
type CostTracker struct {
    mu       sync.Mutex
    sessions map[string]*SessionCost
}

type SessionCost struct {
    Agents    map[string]*AgentCost
    StartTime time.Time
}

type AgentCost struct {
    InputTokens  int
    OutputTokens int
    Model        string
}

## Pricing Table
var modelPricing = map[string]struct{ InputPer1K, OutputPer1K float64 }{
    'claude-opus':   {0.015, 0.075},
    'claude-sonnet': {0.003, 0.015},
    'gpt-4o':        {0.005, 0.015},
    'gemini-pro':    {0.00025, 0.0005},
}

## Key Methods
func (t *CostTracker) RecordPrompt(session, pane string, prompt string)
func (t *CostTracker) RecordResponse(session, pane string, response string)
func (t *CostTracker) GetSessionCost(session string) float64

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tracks input/output tokens per agent
- Calculates cost based on model
- Thread-safe operation
- Persists across session restart

## Technical Notes
Use tiktoken-go for token estimation
Store in .ntm/costs.json","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1qusl","title":"process_triage: Implement rano correlation for improved accuracy","description":"Implement rano correlation for improved process_triage accuracy.

## Purpose

Correlate network activity from rano with process health status to improve accuracy of stuck agent detection.

## Correlation Logic

1. Agent has network activity -> likely not stuck
2. No network for 5+ minutes -> check process state
3. Combine signals for confidence score

## Acceptance Criteria

1. rano data fetched for correlation
2. Network activity correlated with process state
3. Confidence scores improved with rano data
4. Fallback when rano unavailable
5. Unit tests for correlation logic
6. E2E test with real rano data","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:23:10.937120449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:07:30.124431333Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qusl","depends_on_id":"bd-1es69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qusl","depends_on_id":"bd-27l8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qusl","depends_on_id":"bd-lzvh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qv92","title":"Implement soft exit sequence with precise Ctrl-C timing","description":"Implement the soft exit sequence using Ctrl-C with 0.1s timing gap.

## Critical Timing
- Send first Ctrl-C
- Wait exactly 0.1 seconds (100ms)
- Send second Ctrl-C
- Wait up to 3 seconds for exit

IMPORTANT: Slower timing (e.g., 2 seconds) does NOT trigger claude code exit!

## Gotchas
1. Use /usr/bin/tmux not tmux (zsh plugin conflict)
2. Target format is session:1.N (window is 1, not 0)
3. Pane 1 is control shell, agent panes start at 2","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:24:48.717645764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:41.443100371Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:41.442992527Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qv92","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
